name: Rudy Wiedoeft
abstract: Rudolph Cornelius Wiedoeft (January 3, 1893 – February 18, 1940) was an American saxophonist whose compositions and solos on recordings helped popularize the instrument.

concerts.label: New York Building Superintendents Association Minstrel/Vaudeville Program

about_quotes.speaker: Bruce Johnstone
about_quotes.evidence: he had been a professional musician in England and he’d recorded all of Rudy Wiedoeft’s material on baritone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bruce Johnstone interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: he had a lot to do with me emulating him and so forth... So Rudy Wiedoeft meant a lot to me, yes. I emulated him
about_quotes.doc_title: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Norwich, New York, November 14, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: although he certainly wouldn't have been considered as jazz oriented as somebody like Johnny Hodges or Hilton Jefferson or Happy Haddie Caldwell, anybody like that.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: My father bought me Rudy Wiedoeft records... I’ll never forget the first time I heard Rudy Wiedoeft play
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Oneonta, New York, December 28, 2005

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: I mean Coleman Hawkins had to have listened to Paul [inaudible] and Rudy what’s his name —
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023
